scope: Read-only git working tree inventory, classification, suspicious-file audit, duplicate/superseded artifact audit, commit grouping, and command pack. No DB/pipeline/canon work performed.

POSSIBLE_MISPLACED_FILES.reason: Executable writes JustTCG/external mapping data and matches existing backend/pricing mapping-worker convention. Prize Pack ready-batch scripts reference this path directly.

README was requested with warehouse worker docs because Prize Pack batch closure calls the pricing worker from the warehouse flow. The README explicitly documents the executable path under backend/pricing.
Identity-slot audit is read-only identity-domain logic and belongs under backend/identity.
README is colocated with its identity executable.
One-purpose source acquisition normalizer writes warehouse checkpoint JSON and is scoped to Prize Pack warehouse evidence.
Migration path and naming match repo convention. It is schema work and should not be bundled with docs or runtime-only commits.

POSSIBLE_DUPLICATE_OR_SUPERSEDED_FILES.reason: Failed/direct Series 2 acquisition attempts are superseded by the official fallback acquisition, but they document why the fallback path was needed.
Candidate files are exact input manifests proving bounded READY batch execution. Closure files supersede them operationally but do not replace them historically.
Evidence input and target-slice artifacts prove how each bounded slice was selected.
Route repair input, cluster, and target-cluster artifacts are needed to reproduce structural repair decisions.
Subset and residue files document bounded partial closure and should not be removed without a separate artifact-retention policy.

FILES_REQUIRING_HUMAN_DECISION.reason: Human should decide migration apply/staging timing. Intent is clear enough for SEPARATE_COMMIT classification, but schema deployment is operationally distinct from repo packaging.

misplaced_files_summary.reason: Executable writes JustTCG/external mapping data and matches existing backend/pricing mapping-worker convention. Prize Pack ready-batch scripts reference this path directly.

README was requested with warehouse worker docs because Prize Pack batch closure calls the pricing worker from the warehouse flow. The README explicitly documents the executable path under backend/pricing.
Identity-slot audit is read-only identity-domain logic and belongs under backend/identity.
README is colocated with its identity executable.
One-purpose source acquisition normalizer writes warehouse checkpoint JSON and is scoped to Prize Pack warehouse evidence.
Migration path and naming match repo convention. It is schema work and should not be bundled with docs or runtime-only commits.

duplicate_or_superseded_summary.reason: Failed/direct Series 2 acquisition attempts are superseded by the official fallback acquisition, but they document why the fallback path was needed.
Candidate files are exact input manifests proving bounded READY batch execution. Closure files supersede them operationally but do not replace them historically.
Evidence input and target-slice artifacts prove how each bounded slice was selected.
Route repair input, cluster, and target-cluster artifacts are needed to reproduce structural repair decisions.
Subset and residue files document bounded partial closure and should not be removed without a separate artifact-retention policy.

migration_status.reason: The file adds warehouse candidate identity audit columns and transition guard constraints. It is structurally related to the warehouse identity milestone, but it is schema work and should be committed separately from runtime, checkpoints, and docs. It was not changed by this cleanup pass.

files_needing_human_decision.reason: Human should decide migration apply/staging timing. Intent is clear enough for SEPARATE_COMMIT classification, but schema deployment is operationally distinct from repo packaging.

commit_groups.name: Runtime / Identity / Warehouse code
commit_groups.why: Runtime workers, helper modules, source acquisition scripts, mapping worker, and tests required by the warehouse/stamped/Prize Pack milestone behavior.
commit_groups.safe_commit_message: Add Prize Pack warehouse identity and evidence workers

commit_groups.name: Contracts and core docs
commit_groups.why: Binding contract docs and contract index changes that govern the runtime behavior.
commit_groups.safe_commit_message: Document stamped identity and Prize Pack contracts

commit_groups.name: Checkpoints / milestone artifacts
commit_groups.why: Reproducible checkpoint chain, source imports, route repairs, evidence passes, ready batch closures, final state, and this cleanup audit.
commit_groups.safe_commit_message: Checkpoint Prize Pack backlog milestone state

commit_groups.name: Playbook pack / worker READMEs
commit_groups.why: Operational playbooks, machine-readable worker index, bucket definitions, source guide, and worker/family documentation.
commit_groups.safe_commit_message: Add Prize Pack backlog execution playbooks

commit_groups.name: Migration
commit_groups.why: Schema migration is validly located but should remain a separate review and commit unit.

commit_A: git add -- backend/identity/*.mjs backend/identity/*.test.mjs backend/images/source_image_enrichment_worker_v1.mjs backend/pricing/promote_source_backed_justtcg_mapping_v1.mjs backend/warehouse/*.mjs backend/warehouse/*.py backend/warehouse/*.test.mjs
git commit -m "Add Prize Pack warehouse identity and evidence workers"
commit_B: git commit -m "Document stamped identity and Prize Pack contracts"
commit_C: git commit -m "Checkpoint Prize Pack backlog milestone state"
commit_D: git commit -m "Add Prize Pack backlog execution playbooks"
commit_E: git commit -m "Add warehouse identity reconciliation migration"
do_not_stage_yet: Do not stage Commit E with A-D. Review migration apply timing first.
Do not remove superseded checkpoint inputs/candidates; keep historical unless a separate retention policy is approved.
